title: Mortgage Payment Assistance - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Mortgage Payment Assistance 5 Matching Service Providers Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF ACCESS HOUSING RETENTION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Assists with mortgage and rent payments based on available funding, limited to once every two years per household. Emergency assistance is not available. Eligible applicants are placed on a waitlist. 3630 Aviation Way Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Varies by service, with a focus on individuals and families who are either currently homeless or at risk of becoming homeless Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (541) 494-1210 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION PROGRAM OF EAST CENTRAL OREGON MAIN OFFICE HOMEOWNERSHIP PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about mortgage resources, down payment assistance programs, foreclosure prevention, and default counseling. 211 SE Byers Avenue Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-noon (541) 276-1926 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OPERATION HOMEFRONT CRITICAL FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides critical financial assistance for documented needs. Types of assistance include: auto payment and repair, child and dependent care, critical baby items, dental, essential home items, food assistance, home repairs, medical, moving and relocation, rent and mortgage, travel and transportation, utilities, and vision care. Home repairs can be considered for all post-9/11 wounded service members. Repairs versus replacements can be considered for deployed eligible service members. Number of times a person can receive assistance: -Active duty member with a rank of E1-E6 may receive 3 total lifetime assists -Currently deployed on Title 10 orders and/are receiving hazardous duty pay may receive 5 total lifetime assists -Documented injury, illness, or wound during post 9/11 service may receive 5 total lifetime assists The number of months of assistance that can be received varies on the type of assistance being requested 17319 San Pedro Avenue Suite 505 San Antonio, TX 78232 Eligibility: Must fit one of three criteria: 1) Wounded, Ill, or Injured Criteria Actively served in the United States military post 9/11 AND suffered a post 9/11 wound, illness, or injury in the line of duty. If discharged, must have Honorable or General (Under Honorable Conditions) discharge within 10 years of discharge. 2) Deployed Criteria A service member of rank E1-E6 with DEERS (Defense Enrollment Eligibility Reporting System) eligible/legal dependents AND one of the following criteria's met: -Currently deployed to an overseas location and receiving Hostile Fire Pay -Assigned to a ship that has been at sea for 30+ days and is en route to theater, forward deployed, or otherwise on a mission status, not a training status -On unaccompanied, overseas PCS orders and receiving Hostile Fire Pay -A National Guard member or Reservist on Title 10 orders (not Title 32) who either: Will deploy to an overseas Hostile Fire Pay theater in the next 90 days or has returned from an overseas Hostile Fire Pay theater within the past 180 days 3) General Criteria -An active duty service member of rank E1-E6 with DEERS eligible/legal dependents Hours: Helpline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week, leave a message and a coordinator will call back Intake Coordinators: Monday-Friday 7am-3pm Email (210) 659-7756 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SIUSLAW OUTREACH SERVICES SIUSLAW AREA RESOURCE CENTER COMMUNITY ASSISTANCE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a variety of services including assistance with mortgage, electric, water and heating bills, prescriptions, shower and laundry vouchers, and transportation passes. Provides sleeping bags, tents, tarps, hats, gloves, and hand warmers during the winter season. All services are dependent on available funds. 1536 W 12th Street Florence, OR 97439 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-3:30pm, Friday 10am-4pm Email (541) 997-2816 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TURNING POINT COMMUNITY SERVICE CENTER TURNING POINT COMMUNITY SERVICE CENTER/FOOD PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Hosts a food pantry that also provides rental assistance for eviction prevention when funding is available, utility assistance, rental deposit assistance, prescription expense assistance, clothing, gas vouchers for medical appointments, and a thrift store. Offers application assistance for OHP,SNAP,TANF, LIHEAP, and Community Action Team (CAT) Housing Solutions. Access to faxing, scanning, emailing, and printing services. 220 E Columbia River Highway Clatskanie, OR 97016 Eligibility: Must meet USDA income guidelines for food pantry program Hours: Client Services: Monday 10am-12pm, Tuesday/Thursday 10am-4pm Food Pantry: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday 11am-2:30pm Thrift Store: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday 10am-4pm Email (503) 728-3126 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ACCESS HOUSING RETENTION PROGRAM COMMUNITY ACTION PROGRAM OF EAST CENTRAL OREGON HOMEOWNERSHIP PROGRAM SIUSLAW OUTREACH SERVICES COMMUNITY ASSISTANCE SERVICES TURNING POINT COMMUNITY SERVICE CENTER TURNING POINT COMMUNITY SERVICE CENTER/FOOD PANTRY
